codeReview.summary: The feature handoff claims commit 5854f064 proves real Rust enum roundtrip behavior, but that commit contains no product or test-code changes and HEAD still uses struct stand-ins in the targeted plain Store regression. As a result, the claimed VAL-ER-001/003/004 proof remains absent.

issues.description: plain_store_enum_fields_roundtrip_through_save_get_list_and_save_many still persists ItPlainStatus and ItPayloadState, which are struct wrappers carrying kind/alue fields rather than actual Rust enums. That means the test does not prove real enum discriminant decoding, Option<Enum> handling, or payload-enum persistence through save/get/list/save_many, so VAL-ER-001, VAL-ER-003, and VAL-ER-004 remain unproven.

The reviewed commit only rewrites existing validation JSON files and does not modify the Rust workspace at all, so it cannot satisfy a fix feature whose description promises replacement of struct stand-ins with actual enum-based proofs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker reported success for a fix feature without adding or updating any failing-first regression and without noticing that the referenced commit changed only validation artifacts. The current rust-library-worker guidance allows verification-only handoffs when a feature appears already present, but it does not force a diff-level sanity check that the cited commit actually touches the promised code/test surface.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl session 5a49229c-8062-44d6-9b26-dd2c49d38e13 shows only read/grep/test commands plus a clean-tree handoff, while git show 5854f064c65dda6e248ccc40c573bea7918314dc changes only .factory/validation/m3-roundtrip-hardening/** JSON files.

summary: Fail. The supposed fix commit does not touch the enum proof tests, and the plain Store regression still uses struct stand-ins instead of real Rust enums. The original scrutiny failure is therefore not addressed.
